parameters.content:
## Grant discovery scrape (F73 kb:grant-discovery-currents)

Reads a Reckons.AI grant-seeker profile, searches Grants.gov for matching CALLS, and appends each match to `knowledge.pending.jsonl` in your synced Drive folder — where Reckons.AI imports it as a pending fact for review.

**Before enabling:**
1. Set the profile URL (Fetch profile node) to your published graph, e.g. `https://<your-site>/knowledge.ttl`, or a Drive/HTTP source of the profile TTL.
2. Set the Google Drive credential + the `knowledge.pending.jsonl` file ID (Append node).
3. Tune the schedule + Grants.gov keywords.

The scrape/schedule live here in n8n; Reckons.AI stays local-first and just imports the results.

nodes.name: Fetch profile TTL
nodes.notes: GET your published knowledge.ttl (F72 kb:published-ttl) or any TTL source of the grant-seeker profile.

nodes.name: Search Grants.gov
nodes.notes: Grants.gov public search. Swap for a foundation/RFP source or the Simpler.Grants.gov API as needed.

parameters.content:
### Append step

Reckons.AI drains `knowledge.pending.jsonl` from your synced folder/Drive (`drainWorkspacePending`).

This node writes the JSONL. For **append** semantics, either:
- Google Drive: download the file → concatenate → update (2 nodes), or
- Read/Write Files From Disk (append) if n8n shares the synced folder.

Wire your credential + the pending.jsonl file location here.

nodes.name: Append to knowledge.pending.jsonl
nodes.notes: Append to the pending file in the folder Reckons.AI syncs. Swap for a Google Drive update if you sync via Drive.
